results.justification: All pass criteria met: output is valid JSON with no markdown fences; business_rules has 7 entries (≥5), each with id and rule fields; acceptance_criteria has 7 entries (≥5), each with given/when/then structure; edge_cases has 6 entries (≥2); confidence object has numeric level (92). Rules cover null handling (BR-6), zero-total behavior (BR-3), and are domain-specific to passbar segmentation. Acceptance criteria cover mixed counts (AC-1), all-fail (AC-2), all-zero/null (AC-3), and all-pass (AC-4).

observations.text: Business rules are highly domain-specific with invariant flags and justifications — well beyond minimum requirements
Acceptance criteria cover all required cases (mixed, all-fail, all-zero, all-pass, null) plus extras like equal-split and extreme ratios
Edge cases are genuinely non-obvious: negative counts, extreme ratios (10000:1), single-count runs
Includes ubiquitous_language with precise definitions grounded in the actual codebase (SQLite nullability, CSS class names)
Confidence reasoning identifies specific low-confidence areas (min visible width for tiny segments) showing honest self-assessment
AC-7 assumes flex:1 rounding strategy which is an implementation detail — acceptance criteria should be implementation-agnostic

results.agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"The eval dashboard is an internal tool for tracking AI agent evaluation runs. The passbar is a visual progress bar on each run row that communicates at-a-glance how an eval run performed across pass/partial/fail outcomes. Moving from a single-color bar to a multi-segment bar provides immediate visual breakdown without requiring users to read numeric counts.","bounded_context":"Eval Dashboard — visualization of evaluation run results.
